product_overview: AlleleA1 is a browser-based simulation app for exploring how allele frequencies change over generations under different evolutionary forces. Users can enter genetic parameters, run simulations, and compare predicted versus observed frequency trends across scenarios.
core_goals: Model changes in allele frequencies across generations
Test how fitness values affect genotype and allele outcomes
Compare dominant, recessive, lethal, and balanced scenarios
Explore mutation-driven shifts in allele frequency
Reset the simulation to analyze a new scenario

features.name: Set Initial Allele Frequency
features.description: Users configure the starting frequency of an allele before running a simulation.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter an initial allele frequency -> Run the simulation -> See allele frequency changes over generations

features.name: Adjust Genotype Fitness
features.description: Users set fitness values for the three genotypes to model selection effects.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter fitness values for each genotype -> Run the simulation -> See allele frequencies shift according to selection
Navigate to / -> Enter unequal fitness values -> Observe one allele increase while the other declines -> Compare the resulting trend with the expected outcome

features.name: Compare Dominant and Recessive Allele Behavior
features.description: Users test how a rare allele changes when it behaves as dominant or recessive under selection.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Set a rare initial allele frequency -> Enter fitness values that make the allele dominant -> Run the simulation -> See the rare allele increase rapidly
Navigate to / -> Set a rare initial allele frequency -> Enter fitness values that make the allele recessive -> Run the simulation -> See the rare allele increase slowly
Navigate to / -> Set a common initial allele frequency -> Enter fitness values that make the allele dominant -> Run the simulation -> See the allele approach fixation
Navigate to / -> Set a common initial allele frequency -> Enter fitness values that make the allele recessive -> Run the simulation -> See the allele reach fixation faster

features.name: Model Recessive Disease Prevalence and Selection
features.description: Users estimate genotype frequencies for a recessive disease and test selection against affected individuals.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Set the disease allele frequency -> Observe the genotype distribution under Hardy-Weinberg expectations -> Estimate how many individuals are affected, carriers, and unaffected
Navigate to / -> Set fitness of affected individuals lower than the others -> Run the simulation -> See the disease allele decline over generations
Navigate to / -> Apply selection against affected individuals -> Observe that carriers preserve the allele -> See the disease allele decline but persist in the population

features.name: Analyze Viable versus Lethal Alleles
features.description: Users simulate a viable dominant allele competing against a lethal recessive allele.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Set the initial allele frequency to a midrange value -> Enter fitness values where the lethal genotype has zero fitness -> Run the simulation -> See the viable allele rise toward fixation
Navigate to / -> Adjust heterozygote fitness downward while keeping the lethal genotype at zero fitness -> Run the simulation -> See the allele settle at a stable intermediate frequency
Navigate to / -> Change the initial allele frequency to a high value -> Run the simulation -> See the population converge to the same equilibrium frequency

features.name: Test Mutation-Only Change
features.description: Users explore how mutation alone shifts allele frequencies across generations.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Set a starting allele frequency -> Enter a one-way mutation rate from one allele to the other -> Run the simulation -> See the source allele decrease gradually and the target allele increase
Navigate to / -> Set mutation in the reverse direction to zero -> Run the simulation -> See the trend continue in one direction without reversal

features.name: Model Mutation-Selection Balance for Rare Alleles
features.description: Users estimate mutation rates needed to maintain a rare deleterious allele at observed population levels.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Set the deleterious allele frequency -> Enter low fitness for affected individuals -> Run the simulation -> See the allele decline strongly under selection
Navigate to / -> Adjust the mutation rate upward -> Run the simulation -> See the allele stabilize near the observed frequency
Navigate to / -> Compare the estimated balancing mutation rate with the reported biological mutation rate -> Observe whether mutation alone appears sufficient to maintain the allele

features.name: Reset for New Scenario
features.description: Users clear the current setup and begin a new simulation from scratch.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Complete a simulation -> Select the option to start a new download -> See the input reset to an empty state -> Enter a new scenario

routes.description: Main page with video URL input, format selection, and download functionality

features.name: Paste Video URL
features.description: User pastes a video URL from clipboard or types it manually to begin downloading
user_interactions: Click paste button to auto-paste from clipboard
Type or paste a video URL into the input field
Click the Fetch Video Info button or press Enter
See loading animation while fetching
See error toast if URL is invalid

features.name: View Video Information
features.description: After fetching, user sees video thumbnail, title, uploader, duration, and platform badge
user_interactions: View video thumbnail preview
View video title and uploader name
View video duration
View platform badge (YouTube, Instagram, etc.)

features.name: Quick Download
features.description: One-click download at preset quality levels (Best, 4K, 1080p, 720p, 480p, 360p, Audio)
user_interactions: Click a quick download quality card (e.g. Best Quality, 1080p, 720p)
Choose save location in Save As dialog
See progress bar with download speed, downloaded size, and percentage
See completion checkmark after download finishes

features.name: Advanced Format Selection
features.description: View and download specific video formats with codec, resolution, fps, and filesize details
user_interactions: Click Show All Formats (Advanced) toggle
Switch between Combined, Video Only, and Audio Only tabs
View format details (resolution, codec, fps, bitrate, file size)
Click Download button on a specific format
Choose save location in Save As dialog
See progress bar during download

features.name: New Download
features.description: Reset the UI and start over with a new video URL
user_interactions: Click the New Download button
UI resets to initial state with empty input field

known_limitations.issue: showSaveFilePicker API only works in Chromium browsers (Chrome, Edge). Firefox falls back to blob download without save location prompt.
known_limitations.impact: Firefox users won't get the Save As dialog but downloads still work

known_limitations.issue: Some platforms may not provide Content-Length headers, so progress percentage won't be available
known_limitations.impact: Progress bar shows indeterminate state instead of percentage
